observations.label: account_b_signup
observations.value: operator completed Account B signup/auth locally; Hermes observed only post-auth UI state after completion

observations.label: primary_team
observations.value: onboarding step 1 selected Customer Support as low-risk non-secret profile customization

observations.label: channels_tools
observations.value: step 2 listed Email/Chat/Slack/WhatsApp/Voice/SMS/Social media/Other and prior support-tool yes/no; skipped without connecting external channels

observations.label: shared_inbox_creation
observations.value: step 3 created default owned shared inbox labels Support and Support - Priority from recommended setup; no customer data or external channel connected

observations.label: shared_email_connection
observations.value: step 4 offered Gmail Account and Office 365 User Mailbox connection plus channel settings link; skipped/set up later; current dashboard still prompts Connect your first shared channel

observations.label: remaining_tasks
observations.value: setup guide marks first shared inbox completed and lists Connect shared channel, Automate workflows/rules, Discover Topics, Invite team as remaining gates

observations.label: left_navigation
observations.value: visible owned Account B workspace surfaces include Open/Drafts/Later/Done/More, Shared Customer Support with Support and Support - Priority, Sandbox, demo Support/Analytics/Live Chat, setup guide, trial banner

observations.label: settings_hover
observations.value: settings icon visible; passive click/hover did not open settings page in captured state; no setting mutation occurred

positive_evidence: Account B can reach authenticated <program-name> dashboard after operator completed signup/auth gate.
Owned shared inbox labels were created by onboarding: Customer Support group with Support and Support - Priority visible in left nav.
External channel connection remains a visible hard gate, not completed.
Setup guide exposes remaining proof-relevant surfaces without needing external communication: channel connect, rules/workflows, Topics, team invite.
negative_controls: No Gmail/Office 365/OAuth/mailbox/channel connection was initiated or completed.
No invite, outbound message, discussion, comment, workflow/rule activation, API token creation, API call, scanner/fuzzer/DAST, or report submission was performed.
Visible inbox state showed inbox zero/no conversations; no customer/non-owned data was accessed.
Screenshots are kept in ignored local storage only and must be redacted before sharing because browser/account labels may be visible.
redactions: Do not record or share phone numbers, OTPs, passwords, cookies, tokens, API keys, verification links, or raw account alias values.
Treat screenshots with account initials/names, workspace labels, browser URL IDs, and trial/account banners as local-only unless separately redacted.
Use labels Account A, Account B, owned workspace, owned shared inbox rather than raw identifiers in broad handoff artifacts.
candidate_signals: Shared inbox membership/visibility and default recommended inbox creation are worth preserving as a future owned A/B permission-candidate family.
Rules/workflow automation and Topics are proof-relevant but remain blocked until explicit approval for state-changing activation or adequate passive-only documentation mapping.
Team invite remains a high-value boundary candidate but blocked without explicit invite/role action approval and owned recipient plan.
Channel/OAuth/API/UI mismatch families remain blocked until explicit token/channel handling and redaction plan exists.
blocked_states: Topics activation dependent on connected shared channel
Report submission or report-ready evidence promotion
next_learning_seed: <program-name> Account B authenticated dashboard is surface_only: onboarding created owned shared inbox labels and exposed channel/workflow/topics/invite gates. Next safe work is passive UI/docs mapping or an operator-approved owned A/B proof boundary; no external channel/invite/workflow/API/report actions.
